data.body: Investigation (agent-afb23256): Bug is theoretical. Empirically verified stub routing: case fallthrough '*) exec /bin/bash "$@"' correctly preserves ALL args (flag-first included). Tested: stub called with '-x /tmp/target.sh foo bar' correctly executes /bin/bash with -x tracing on target.sh — no misrouting. Production code in scripts/create-dso-app.sh calls bash in two ways: 'bash --version' (handled explicitly) and 'bash "$_setup_script" "$project_dir" "$resolved_plugin_root"' (script-path first, $1 = script path). Neither passes leading flags. The only misrouting risk would be if a future case branch parsed $1 as a script path, but the current code does not. Recommendation: keep ticket open for visibility as a latent test-double fragility, or close as won't-fix/theoretical. No code fix applied per fix-bug policy on theoretical bugs.
